evals.name: yagni-pushback
evals.expected_output: Pushes back on the premature abstraction. Cites YAGNI — only TaskService needs events right now. Suggests a simpler alternative: inline event handling or Node.js built-in EventEmitter for the single use case. Does not approve building the generic base class for hypothetical future needs.

assertions.description: Explicitly references YAGNI or 'You Aren't Gonna Need It' as the reason to avoid the abstraction
Does NOT approve proceeding with the generic EventEmitter base class
Suggests a simpler alternative (e.g., Node.js built-in EventEmitter, inline callbacks, or just direct function calls)
Points out that only one service currently needs events, making the abstraction premature

evals.name: goal-first-check
evals.prompt: Hey, while I'm working on the JWT auth, I noticed our error messages are inconsistent. Some endpoints return { error: 'message' } and others return { message: 'error text', code: 500 }. Let me quickly standardize all error responses across the API before continuing with auth.
evals.expected_output: Questions whether standardizing error responses is aligned with the current goal. Acknowledges the inconsistency but asks whether this side-track serves the 'Now' milestone (JWT auth). Suggests noting it for later rather than context-switching.

assertions.description: Questions whether the error standardization task aligns with the current goal or milestone
Does NOT simply start implementing the error standardization without questioning it
Suggests deferring the side-task (e.g., noting it for later, adding to Next milestone, or finishing auth first)

evals.name: fail-fast-enforcement
evals.expected_output: Flags two violations: (1) the TODO comment — must be resolved before committing, not deferred; (2) the empty catch block that silently swallows auth errors and calls next() — this lets unauthenticated requests through. Demands immediate fixes for both, citing Fail Fast philosophy.

assertions.description: Flags the TODO comment as a violation that must be resolved before committing
Flags the catch block that silently swallows errors and calls next() — allowing unauthenticated requests through
References Fail Fast philosophy or principle as the reason these are violations
Demands immediate fixes rather than accepting the code as-is
